steps.title: Spot the defining claim
steps.content: The second clause reveals what the blank must describe: “we will never be able to answer it”—meaning the question is unanswerable.

steps.title: Match the word to that requirement
steps.content:
The blank needs to mean unanswerable — something we can never resolve. Substitute each choice:
  • Choice A — irresolvable: "the question is irresolvable" — incapable of being settled or answered. The passage says "we will never be able to answer it," so this fits precisely. ✓
  • Choice B — self-contradictory: "the question is self-contradictory" — internally inconsistent, containing a logical conflict. The passage gives no hint of internal contradiction; it's unresolvable because evidence is gone forever. ✗
  • Choice C — imperative: "the question is imperative" — urgent or essential. The passage explicitly says the opposite: we can never answer it, not that we must. ✗
  • Choice D — unavoidable: "the question is unavoidable" — impossible to escape or ignore. But the passage's point is that we can't answer it, not that we can't avoid asking it. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
